methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Hexana is a seed-stage nuclear energy company that has raised €21M to date, founded by Gabriel Lévy. The valuation universe comprises 11 nuclear energy peers: three public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and eight private firms including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

The peer-median method is inapplicable to this valuation. The nuclear energy sector contains only eight private comparables, but none satisfy the screening criteria for a robust peer set. There are zero seed-stage same-cohort peers, only one adjacent-stage peer, and zero similar-valuation-tier matches—falling short of the minimum three datapoints required for statistical reliability. Constructing a median from all eight nuclear energy peers regardless of stage and scale would introduce material distortion, as funding stages and company maturity vary significantly across the sector.

The absence of adequate narrow-stage peers reflects the nascent state of private nuclear innovation financing. Without triangulation via peer multiples, alternative valuation frameworks—such as DCF analysis anchored to technology milestones, revenue projections, or cost-of-capital benchmarks—would be required to establish a defensible valuation range. The primary uncertainty is calibrating appropriate discount rates and terminal assumptions for early-stage nuclear technology ventures given limited comparable exits or revenue-generating precedents in the private market.
